purpose: Machine-readable contract for authentication, trial start, purchase recovery, sync scope, canonical account reads, idempotent learning events, default sync strategy, and offline behavior.

default_sync_strategy.learning_state_rule: latest_valid_learning_event_wins_per_card; learning and review completion aggregates plus per-card learning state are derived from accepted events while physical-space and check-in state keep separate authority
default_sync_strategy.physical_space_rule: latest_valid_explicit_space_action_wins unless it violates box knowledge ownership or contract constraints; invalid actions are rejected and client refreshes from server canonical state
default_sync_strategy.physical_space_snapshot_reconciliation: clients preserve each explicit action timestamp, read account-level server canonical state before restored-state push, and accept the canonical merged response; day_key is batch metadata rather than the canonical partition key
default_sync_strategy.physical_space_write_atomicity: CloudBase account-level canonical reads, legacy migration, merge, and write-back run inside one database transaction with conflict retry

canonical_read.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; bootstrap never accepts phone_number in its query or body
canonical_read.missing_state_rule: return explicit empty canonical state for a valid account/day/track; never copy device-local state into a canonical read
canonical_read.cursor_rule: return null when the service has no stored scheduler cursor; do not infer exact cross-device position from device-local state or claim exact resume
canonical_read.consistency_rule: generated_at binds one response and each mutable component exposes acknowledged_at freshness; the current CloudBase multi-collection adapter does not claim a serializable cross-collection transaction
canonical_read.content_rule: content version is the SHA-256 of the normalized ordered source; production requires a matching published content-release.v1 descriptor while development may return release_id null
canonical_read.release_publication_rule: the development importer rejects non-null release descriptors; a separate publication pipeline must verify formal content approval before writing content-release.v1
canonical_read.client_rule: an authenticated client may use validated cached state while offline, but must reconcile with this server read before pushing restored state; without previously validated canonical state and required content, mobile keeps a valid session retryable but blocks learning, local persistence of the reconciled snapshot, mutation replay, and product-state writes until bootstrap succeeds and the loaded card-source content version matches
canonical_read.known_gap: legacy v1 learning and daily snapshots remain migration inputs; learning-events.v2 is contract-defined but its endpoint, client adoption, server projections, and scheduler are not implemented

endpoint.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; reject phone_number or any client-supplied account identity

product_alignment.aggregate_rule: learning and review completion counts plus per-card learning state are derived from accepted immutable events; clients never submit canonical counters or learning snapshots

request_contract.batch_rule: one track per request; require one or more events up to a server-configured bound; preserve input order in the acknowledgement
request_contract.schema_rule: reject unknown fields at the request, event, and device_cursor levels

event_contract.event_id_rule: generate one opaque stable event_id before durable local enqueue and reuse it unchanged for every retry
event_contract.content_version_rule: bind every event to the exact SHA-256 content version used for the answer; the server validates the track, card, interaction, and retained published release in production
event_contract.client_time_rule: preserve a valid RFC3339 client_occurred_at for bounded activity-day attribution, but never use the client clock as membership, entitlement, idempotency, or canonical ordering authority

device_cursor.device_id_rule: use a pseudonymous installation identifier that contains no account or credential material
device_cursor.sequence_rule: use a positive monotonically increasing safe integer allocated before durable enqueue; gaps and out-of-order replay are valid
device_cursor.authority_rule: device_cursor proves replay provenance and detects forks; it is not the scheduler cursor and does not promise exact cross-device resume

idempotency_and_atomicity.exact_replay: return duplicate with the original server_sequence and do not mutate projections
idempotency_and_atomicity.event_id_conflict: the same event key with a different canonical payload rejects the entire request with HTTP 409
idempotency_and_atomicity.device_cursor_conflict: the same device cursor bound to a different event_id rejects the entire request with HTTP 409
idempotency_and_atomicity.mixed_batch_rule: exact duplicates and unseen valid events may commit together and each receives an explicit result
idempotency_and_atomicity.validation_atomicity: any malformed, unauthorized, unknown-content, or conflicting event rejects the entire request before writes
idempotency_and_atomicity.transaction_atomicity: new immutable events, account server sequences, and derived learning projections commit in one transaction; failure leaves no partial acceptance

server_authority.server_sequence_rule: assign one stable monotonically increasing account-scoped server_sequence to each newly accepted event
server_authority.duplicate_sequence_rule: an exact duplicate returns the previously assigned server_sequence
server_authority.canonical_ordering_rule: server_sequence is canonical ordering; client time and device sequence never override it
server_authority.per_card_state_rule: the highest valid server_sequence for a card is the canonical latest learning event
server_authority.activity_day_rule: derive the China product activity day from validated client_occurred_at within configured retention and future-skew bounds; otherwise reject the event rather than trust an arbitrary day_key
server_authority.entitlement_rule: event acceptance never grants membership or content access; access remains server-authoritative and content delivery remains independently gated

acknowledgement_contract.retry_rule: a missing or ambiguous response must be retried with byte-equivalent canonical event payloads and the same event IDs
acknowledgement_contract.queue_removal_rule: remove an event from the local queue only after an accepted or duplicate result for that event_id
acknowledgement_contract.canonical_refresh_rule: after replay, read /v2/bootstrap again before presenting reconciled server state or sending dependent mutations

migration_boundary.legacy_v1_role: development-only migration input; v1 daily and learning snapshots are never accepted as learning-events.v2 payloads
migration_boundary.production_rule: production continues to reject all v1 routes
migration_boundary.launch_claim_rule: a green contract PR does not satisfy canonical-bootstrap-and-idempotent-events, server-scheduler, formal content approval, or launch readiness
